name: Rocket (Rust Web Framework)

performance.notes: Highly performant; async in 0.5.x via tokio

agent_readiness.notes: Macro-driven Rust web framework. #[macro_use] extern crate rocket. #[get('/')] fn index() -> &'static str { 'Hello' }. #[launch] fn rocket() -> _ { rocket::build().mount('/', routes![index]) }. #[post('/user', data = '<user>')] fn create(user: Json<User>) -> Json<User>. Request guards: #[get('/admin')] fn admin(_auth: AdminGuard). State: rocket::State<Config>. Fairings (middleware): .attach(Fairing). #[catch(404)] fn not_found() -> &'static str. JSON: rocket::serde::json::Json<T>. Forms: Form<T>. async handlers natively in 0.5.x. Multipart: TempFile. Responders: status::Created<Json<T>>. Route params: #[get('/item/<id>')] fn get(id: i32). Query params: #[get('/?<page>')] fn list(page: u32).

gotchas: 0.4.x was nightly-only — 0.5.x stable but migration required
Macro-heavy API — compile errors can be cryptic
Less ecosystem adoption than Axum in 2025
Request guards require FromRequest impl — boilerplate
best_when: Ergonomic Rust web APIs with macro-driven routing
Teams preferring convention-over-configuration Rust web dev
When Axum's explicitness is too verbose
avoid_when: When OpenAPI spec generation is required
summary: Rocket is a macro-driven Rust web framework. #[get('/')] fn index() -> &'static str. #[launch] fn rocket() -> _ { rocket::build().mount('/', routes![index]) }. JSON<T>, Form<T>, State<T>, request guards. async in 0.5.x. MIT/Apache-2.0. Best for ergonomic convention-driven Rust web APIs. Less ecosystem than Axum. No built-in OpenAPI. 0.4 nightly-only migration needed.
